description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a large crustacean, likely a prawn or shrimp, with a segmented body and prominent anatomical features. The exoskeleton is a mix of brown and greenish hues, with a glossy, hard surface typical of aquatic arthropods. Two large, dark claws extend from the front, each with a curved, robust appearance, while multiple slender walking legs are visible along the sides of the thorax. The abdomen is elongated and tapers towards a fan-shaped tail, and long, thin antennae project forward from the head. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the cephalothorax, giving the crustacean a wide field of view.
description: A large, brown-green crustacean with prominent claws, segmented body, long antennae, and a glossy exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, claws, and antennae, with the segmentation and tail clearly visible.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the symmetry of the claws, the arrangement of walking legs, and the tapering abdomen.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the claws and antennae extending outward and the body segmentation visible.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the large claws, the spread of the walking legs, and the forward-projecting antennae.
